features.label: WHY CODY MASTER
features.title:
Everything you need,
in a single kit
features.subtitle: No coding required. No need to hire developers. AI handles everything from A to Z.

cards.title: 10x Faster
cards.description: From idea to a complete website in minutes. AI codes, designs, and deploys automatically — you just say what you want.

cards.title: Pro Quality
cards.description: Production-ready code — not throwaway prototypes. 30+ skills ensure quality from security to SEO.

cards.title: Save 90%
cards.description: Compared to hiring agencies or freelancers, you save both time and money. One kit replaces an entire team.

cards.title: Dead Simple
cards.description: Chat naturally, AI understands and delivers. No complex interfaces, no learning curve.

usp.label: WHAT MAKES US DIFFERENT
usp.title:
Not just another AI tool.
This is a design-first engine.
usp.subtitle: Cody Master doesn't just write code — it designs, previews, and respects your existing design system.

cards.title: Kanban Dashboard
cards.description: Manage your entire project visually. Track tasks, skill pipelines, and deployments in real-time — all from one command center.

cards.title: Preview Design Before Code
cards.description: See exactly what your UI will look like before a single line of code is written. Iterate on design with AI — no surprises.

cards.title: Full Design Control
cards.description: Choose your aesthetic — typography, colors, spacing, animations. AI follows YOUR design direction, not random defaults.

cards.title: Works with Your Design System

meta.title: Skill Library — Cody Master Kit

skillsPage.heroSub: Every skill is a dedicated AI expert. Search, filter, and discover the right skill for your task.

catalog.name: cm-brainstorm-idea
catalog.description: Multi-dimensional evaluation using Design Thinking + TRIZ 9 Windows + Double Diamond. Outputs 2-3 qualified options with clear recommendations.
catalog.prompt: Analyze my e-commerce platform and suggest improvements

catalog.name: cm-planning
catalog.description: Explores intent and requirements before implementation. Documents the full plan before any code is written — saving hours of rework.
catalog.prompt: Plan the implementation for user authentication

catalog.name: cm-content-factory
catalog.description: Self-learning content engine with StoryBrand, Cialdini, SUCCESs frameworks. Generates SEO-optimized content for any niche instantly.
catalog.prompt: Write a blog post about remote work productivity

catalog.name: cm-ux-master
catalog.description: 48 UX Laws, 37 Design Tests, 1032+ design patterns. Generates complete design systems, components, and validates accessibility.
catalog.prompt: Design a dashboard for a SaaS analytics app

catalog.name: cm-ui-preview
catalog.description: Generates production-ready UI previews using Google Stitch or Pencil.dev. See the design before writing any code.
catalog.prompt: Preview a mobile app login screen

catalog.name: cm-execution
catalog.description: Batch execution with checkpoints, parallel dispatch for independent tasks. Implements plans efficiently with minimal rework.
catalog.prompt: Execute the implementation plan for the checkout feature

catalog.name: cm-tdd
catalog.description: Write tests before code. Ensures every feature has proper test coverage from the start.
catalog.prompt: Write tests for the payment processing module

catalog.name: cm-debugging
catalog.description: Systematic bug analysis — finds the real cause, not just the symptom. Prevents recurring issues.
catalog.prompt: Debug why the login page crashes on mobile

catalog.name: cm-code-review
catalog.description: Full review lifecycle — request reviews, handle feedback with technical rigor, and complete branch integration.
catalog.prompt: Review the latest pull request for the API module

catalog.name: cm-quality-gate
catalog.description: Enforces test gates, evidence-based verification, and frontend safety checks. No deploy without passing.
catalog.prompt: Run quality gate before deploying to production

catalog.name: cm-test-gate
catalog.description: Sets up reliable test gates for any project — stack detection, core test files, script wiring, and CI patterns.
catalog.prompt: Set up test infrastructure for my Next.js project

catalog.name: cm-terminal
catalog.description: Enforces clear progress logging, output reading, and error-stop behavior. Never leaves terminal processes unchecked.
catalog.prompt: Run and monitor the build process

catalog.name: cm-safe-deploy
catalog.description: Multi-gate deploy pipeline with test gates, build verification, frontend safety checks, and rollback strategy.
catalog.prompt: Deploy the staging build to Cloudflare Pages

catalog.name: cm-project-bootstrap
catalog.description: Starts any project from scratch — design system, staging+production, i18n, SEO, test infrastructure, 8-gate deploy pipeline.
catalog.prompt: Bootstrap a new SaaS project with Next.js

catalog.name: cm-git-worktrees
catalog.description: Creates isolated git worktrees with smart directory selection. Work on features without disturbing your main branch.
catalog.prompt: Create a worktree for the new payment feature

catalog.name: cm-safe-i18n
catalog.description: Multi-pass batching, parallel-per-language dispatch, 8 audit gates, and HTML integrity checks for translations.
catalog.prompt: Translate the app from English to Vietnamese

catalog.name: cm-readit
catalog.description: TTS reading mode, pre-recorded MP3 player, and Voice CRO trigger system. Turn any website into audio-enabled.
catalog.prompt: Add text-to-speech reading mode to my blog

catalog.name: cm-secret-shield
catalog.description: Pre-commit secret scanning, repo-wide pattern detection, token lifecycle management. Prevents leaks at every stage.
catalog.prompt: Scan the repo for exposed API keys

catalog.name: cm-identity-guard
catalog.description: Verifies and locks project identity before any push or deploy. Prevents wrong-account deploys and cross-project leaks.
catalog.prompt: Verify git config before pushing to production

catalog.name: cm-ads-tracker
catalog.description: Generates complete tracking setup: Meta Pixel + CAPI, TikTok Events, Google Ads Enhanced Conversions, UTM attribution.
catalog.prompt: Set up Facebook Pixel and Google Ads tracking

catalog.name: cm-continuity
catalog.description: Maintains context across sessions via CONTINUITY.md. Captures mistakes and learnings to prevent repeating errors.
catalog.prompt: Load context from the previous session

catalog.name: cm-dockit
catalog.description: Analyze codebases, generate Personas, JTBD, Process Flows, SOPs, API references. One scan = complete knowledge base.
catalog.prompt: Generate documentation for my Frappe app

catalog.name: cm-skill-chain
catalog.description: Compose skills into automated pipelines. One task triggers multi-skill workflows with progress tracking.
catalog.prompt: Chain brainstorm → plan → execute for my feature

catalog.name: cm-skill-mastery
catalog.description: Meta-skill — knows when to invoke which skill, how to create new skills, and skill discovery across the kit.
catalog.prompt: Which skill should I use for UX audit?

catalog.name: cm-skill-index
catalog.description: Progressive Disclosure index — 3 layers of skill loading. Efficient context management for AI agents.

catalog.description: Complete guide to the Cody Master skill kit — from idea to deploy. Covers the full workflow and common use cases.

catalog.name: cm-dashboard
catalog.description: Central command center for monitoring skill activity, project status, and progress across tasks.
catalog.prompt: Show me the project dashboard

catalog.name: cm-start
catalog.description: Fastest way to kick off a new task. Auto-detects context and routes to the right skill immediately.
catalog.prompt: Start working on my new feature

catalog.name: cm-status
catalog.description: Quick health check of your project — build status, test coverage, deployment state, and pending tasks.
catalog.prompt: What's the current status of my project?

catalog.description: A template demonstrating the universal skill format compatible with Antigravity, Claude, Cursor, and OpenClaw.

bornFromPain.label: WHY WE BUILT THIS
bornFromPain.title:
Born from real pain,
not a business plan
bornFromPain.subtitle: Every skill in this kit exists because of a real problem that cost real time and real tears.
bornFromPain.quote: "I'm a Product Manager with 10+ years of experience. I can't code. AI gave me 100 hands — but without discipline, those hands created chaos."

cards.title: Design Chaos
cards.pain: AI designed every project differently. Same brand, 3 different styles. Clients thought it was 3 different companies.

cards.title: The Week AI Deleted Everything
cards.pain: During an i18n refactor, AI lost context and deleted a week's worth of code. Had to redo it 4 times. Fix one thing, break another.

cards.title: Great Product, Zero Users
cards.pain: Built amazing apps but nobody knew how to use them. No docs, no onboarding, no self-service. Support team overwhelmed.

From ideas to products,
in every category

cases.title: SaaS Applications

before.title: Without Cody Master
items: AI breaks code when context is lost

meta.title: Our Story — Cody Master Kit
meta.description: The real story behind Cody Master: born from a Product Manager's pain, built through hundreds of sleepless nights.

storyPage.heroSub: Not a pitch. Not marketing. This is what actually happened.
storyPage.founderIntro: I'm not a developer. I'm a Product Manager with 10+ years of experience. Code — the thing I can't do — used to be the tallest wall between me and turning ideas into reality.

chapters.title: When AI Gave Me 100 Hands
chapters.content:
Then AI appeared. Not 2 extra hands. But 100 hands. Suddenly I — someone who couldn't write a single JavaScript function — could build a full-stack application. Landing page? Done. API? Got it. Database schema? AI handles it.

It felt like someone who'd been riding a bicycle their whole life suddenly got to ride a rocket.

But a rocket without a control system is just... a flying bomb.

chapters.title: Design Chaos: Different Every Time
chapters.content:
I started using AI for company projects. Project A: minimalist, sans-serif, 8px radius. Project B — same system, same brand: suddenly AI switched to serif fonts, round buttons, completely different palette. Project C: yet another style entirely.

Every time, a different design. Never consistent.

As a 10+ year Product Manager, this drove me insane. Not because designs were ugly — sometimes each one looked great individually. But put them side by side and it was pure chaos. Business needs consistency. Brand needs a system. Clients looked at 3 pages and thought they were 3 different companies.
chapters.quote: AI is brilliant at design. It's just terrible at remembering the rules.

chapters.title: The Week AI Deleted Everything
chapters.content:
This is the most painful lesson. I will never forget it.

There was a large SaaS project — running in production, needed a full refactor for multi-language, multi-country support. I handed it to AI. It started... fine. Converting this file, translating that string.

Then everything collapsed.

Context got too long, AI started "losing its mind." It fixed one page, broke another. Got Vietnamese working, English disappeared. And the worst part — during one refactor pass, AI deleted nearly all the existing code. An entire week of work. Gone.

I had to redo it. Attempt 1. Attempt 2. Attempt 3. Attempt 4.

Each time, fix one thing, lose another. I sat in front of the screen at 2 AM, staring at endless diff logs, asking myself why I trusted a production system to AI without protection.

I swore: This will never happen again.
chapters.quote: 4 attempts. An entire week lost. That's when I built the multi-layer protection system.

chapters.title: The Lazy Person's Philosophy
chapters.content:
I'll be honest: I'm lazy.

Not lazy about thinking — lazy about doing the same thing over and over. Every new project: set up git, configure deploy, create design system, wire i18n, write README... Every time the same. Every time exhausting.

And I realized: Any problem I've faced twice should become an automated process by the third time.

So I started packaging every painful lesson into a reusable skill. Improve it, refine it, make it reproducible. Next time anyone hits the same wall, they just call one command.
chapters.quote: A problem encountered twice should never be encountered a third time.

chapters.title: Great Products Nobody Knows About
chapters.content:
10+ years as a Product Manager at a SaaS company, one obsession haunted me: Self-onboarding.

Amazing product. Tons of features. But new users came in and... got lost. No proper docs. No user guides. No API reference. Support team drowning in repeat questions.

And when I built my own products without a writing team: documentation became torture. Finish coding, completely exhausted, zero energy left to write a README.

So cm-dockit was born — a documentation powerhouse. It reads code, understands code, and auto-generates: API docs, SOPs, user guides, persona analysis, JTBD mapping, process flows. Multi-language. SEO-optimized. Package your product into world-class documentation without writing a single word.
chapters.quote: The best product in the world is worthless if nobody knows how to use it.

chapters.title: Mission Control for 100 Hands
chapters.content:
When you run multiple AI agents, multiple projects, multiple tasks in parallel to maximize productivity... you quickly lose control. Which task is done? Which agent is running? Which deploy went to production? How many tokens have been spent?

I'm a visual person. I need to SEE everything that's happening.

Everything on one screen: pipeline progress, task queue, token tracker, event log. No digging through logs. No running CLI commands. Open it — look — know the situation instantly.
chapters.quote: If you can't see it, you can't control it.

chapters.title: Gratitude to the Community
chapters.content:
Not everything here came from my head.

I owe the community a tremendous debt.

Superpower, Loki Mode, UX Max Pro... Brilliant skills that amazing people shared with the world — I learned from them, curated them, refined them, and integrated them into the Cody Master ecosystem. Every skill in this toolkit isn't just code — it's collective intelligence.

Thank you to everyone who shared. Truly.
chapters.quote: Standing on the shoulders of a generous community.

closing.title: So What Is Cody Master?
closing.content:
It's not a plugin. Not a prompt template.

It's the answer from a Product Manager who can't code, after hundreds of sleepless nights, to one single question:
closing.question: "How can someone with ideas but no technical skills create world-class products — from landing pages, full-stack apps, documentation, to self-onboarding — without sacrificing quality?"
closing.punchline:
30+ skills. Each skill is a lesson. Each lesson is a sleepless night.

And now, you don't have to go through those nights.
